args.command: cd atlases/cobra && python -c "import json; a=json.load(open('atlas.json')); print(list(a.keys()))"

args.description: List tables in cobra index DB

6111:      "excerpt": "They must either walk the tree programmatically and set `PersistentPreRunE` on the root, or factor middleware into a closure they attach to each command at construction.",
6221:      "excerpt": "it's why cobra has `RunE` as a separate field rather than a parameter on `Run`, and why `SetContext` was added rather than making `ctx` a private field with only a getter",
6255:      "claim": "Runnability is derived from data (Run != nil || RunE != nil), not declared through a type/interface.",
6257:      "excerpt": "`Runnable()` (`command.go:1596-1597`) is literally `return c.Run != nil || c.RunE != nil`. There is no `type Runnable interface`",
6457:      "claim": "A command defined with only Run (no RunE) cannot signal failure except via os.Exit, which bypasses PostRun, PersistentPostRun, and global finalizers.",
6458:      "rationale": "Historical norm but dangerous; documentation nudges new users toward RunE.",
6469:      "claim": "An error from PreRunE skips ValidateRequiredFlags; PreRunE cannot rely on flag validation having occurred.",
6470:      "rationale": "Flag validation happens after PreRunE in the pipeline order.",
6471:      "excerpt": "A `PreRunE` that returns an error short-circuits the pipeline before flag validation.",
6506:      "claim": "Cleanup code that must run after Run regardless of success must use defer inside RunE, not PostRun/PostRunE.",
6507:      "rationale": "PostRun is skipped on RunE failure; this is the most frequent source of production bugs around the hook model.",
6508:      "excerpt": "Any code that must run after `Run` regardless of success — closing files, releasing locks, writing audit logs — belongs in a `defer` inside the `RunE` closure, not in `PostRun`/`PostRunE`.",
6556:      "claim": "PostRun, PostRunE, and PersistentPostRun do NOT execute if Run/RunE returns an error; any pipeline error short-circuits all subsequent hooks.",
6558:      "excerpt": "if `RunE` returns an error, `PostRun` and `PersistentPostRun` do **not** execute.",
6568:      "claim": "RunE takes absolute precedence over Run: if both are set, Run is never called. The same shadowing applies to every Pre/Post E-variant pair.",
6570:      "excerpt": "**RunE takes absolute precedence**: if both `Run` and `RunE` are set, `Run` is never called.",
6616:      "claim": "ValidateArgs runs before PreRun/PreRunE so that PreRun does not see unvalidated argument shapes.",
6628:      "claim": "ValidateRequiredFlags and ValidateFlagGroups run after PreRun, enabling PreRunE to set flag defaults programmatically and satisfy required-flag checks.",
6629:      "rationale": "PreRun is the last chance to set flag defaults; ordering allows cmd.Flags().Set from PreRunE to satisfy validation.",
7173:      "claim": "SetErrPrefix only affects cobra-rendered errors; user-returned errors from RunE must be formatted by the user to match the prefix.",
